Operation 'batchGeolocation' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Summary is clear and action-oriented. However, description lacks critical input details (request body format, expected array structure) and output details (response format, array of results). Missing information about what data fields are returned and how the batch response is structured. Enhanced description clarifies the request format (array of query items), response format (array of geolocation objects), and consistency with individual query operations for better AI agent understanding of batch operation capabilities and constraints.

data.current_description: Query up to 100 IP addresses in a single HTTP request. Rate limited to 15 requests per minute per originating IP.
data.description_suggestion: Geolocate up to 100 IP addresses or domains in a single batch request. Accepts an array of query items in the request body and returns an array of geolocation and network data objects. Rate limited to 15 requests per minute per originating IP. Supports the same geolocation fields as individual queries.
